title: Legacy encryption in AbstractRESTCommand
description: The class AbstractRESTCommand uses legacy TLS configuration and does not validate the server certificate for outbound connections. This file contains all 5 high-level security issues highlighted by static analysis.

title: Non thread-safe date formatter in DateUtils
description: Non thread-safe SimpleDateFormat in DateUtils could cause incorrectly formatted dates in a multithreaded environment. Called during cart availability caching.

title: Hardcoded cookie handling
description: Legacy cookie class appears to support older browsers using an older HTTP cookie specification. Should use default servlet container behaviour.

title: Largest Contentful Paint
description: LCP of 24.8s on mobile and 5.1s on desktop is well above the 2.5s target. The single biggest factor dragging down the performance score, likely due to render-blocking resources and unoptimised images.

title: Layout instability (CLS)
description: Multiple layout shifts detected with a CLS of 0.48 on mobile (target: under 0.1). Elements moving after initial paint, likely due to images without explicit dimensions and dynamically injected content.

title: Unused JavaScript and CSS
description: 380 KiB of unused JavaScript and 92 KiB of unused CSS identified. Removing these could save approximately 2.3 seconds on mobile. Typical of monolithic accelerator storefront bundles.

title: Checkout flow gaps
description: B2B checkout works via OCC for the trade site. The Accelerator checkout adds payment session handling, delivery cost calculation via AJAX, and address selection not yet exposed via OCC.

title: B2B Punchout (cXML)
description: The entire cXML punchout protocol (5 controllers) is Accelerator-only and tightly coupled to the storefront session model. A headless approach would need a dedicated punchout microservice or OCC extension.

title: B2B Quoting workflow
description: Full quote lifecycle (create, edit, apply discount, submit, approve, reject, checkout from quote) has zero OCC coverage. A significant B2B business process.

title: SAML SSO authentication
description: SAML authentication is implemented via storefront filters. No OCC equivalent exists. A headless frontend would need OAuth2/OIDC with the SAP IDP directly.

title: Assisted Service Mode (ASM)
description: Full ASM lifecycle (agent login, customer emulation, cart binding, Customer 360 view) is entirely storefront session-based. Zero OCC coverage.

title: Cart/wishlist import and export
description: CSV/Excel import into saved carts and wishlists, plus export to PDF/Excel. No OCC equivalent exists.

title: Consent management
description: Custom GDPR consent management and cart voucher apply/remove are storefront-only. Net price preference toggle per user is also not in OCC.

title: Promotions and brand pages
description: Dedicated promotions page, brand pages, quick order, category directory, and maintenance mode are all storefront-only. Lower priority as rendering-layer concerns.

title: JDK21 upgrade
description: Mandatory by August 2026. Blocking issues include XSD-to-Java generation library, OAuth migration to Spring Security 6, and EhCache 2.x to 3.x migration. Full scope unknown until blockers resolved.

title: Email library replacement
description: The 2018 email library should be replaced regardless of headless timeline. This is an operational risk on the current platform.

title: Security remediation
description: Fix the SSL/TLS vulnerabilities in the REST integration layer. All issues are in one file and the fix is straightforward.

title: Headless transition planning
description: Findings feed directly into the Discovery phase. The headless readiness analysis, integration inventory, and JDK21 blocking items provide the evidence base for sizing the engagement.

title: Production API keys in source code
description: Production API keys for third-party services are stored directly in the codebase. Move sensitive properties to the SAP Commerce Cloud Portal and blacklist them in HAC.

title: SAP domain URLs exposed
description: Some API calls reference SAP infrastructure domain names directly. Custom domains are already available and could be used more consistently.
